refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated capability to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints), record derived requirements with traceability to source inputs, and maintain a managed baseline history including calibration updates and performance target revisions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional concerns: derivation (analysis/synthesis), recording (data capture), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into analyzable sub-functions while maintaining traceability to worksite inputs and baseline management as acceptance criteria. High safety criticality demands explicit linkage to safety constraints in derivation logic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall demonstrate: (1) derivation algorithm accepts worksite mission inputs and produces documented performance targets; (2) recorded targets include source traceability and timestamp; (3) baseline history is queryable and auditable; (4) calibration updates are logged with change rationale; (5) all outputs are accessible to Site Manager and Safety Manager stakeholders.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context inputs
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and derivation method
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, calibration updates, and requirement revisions with audit trail

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance analytics engine that ingests worksite mission data, applies derivation rules (task type, site conditions, safety constraints), outputs requirement set with traceability metadata, and stores versioned baselines in a managed repository
DP-B: Distributed derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules with a central registry that collects and reconciles derived requirements
DP-C: Manual requirement derivation by Site Manager with template-driven recording and version control system
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance analytics engine that ingests worksite mission data, applies derivation rules (task type, site conditions, safety constraints), outputs requirement set with traceability metadata, and stores versioned baselines in a managed repository

potential_sources: Coupling to worksite input data sources (mission context, site conditions) – mitigated by standardized Tasking interface
Coupling to safety constraint definitions – mitigated by explicit SafetyAlert interface input
Coupling to baseline storage and audit trail – mitigated by encapsulation within single IM boundary
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because derivation logic, recording, and baseline management are co-located in a single functional module with well-defined external interfaces (Tasking in, SafetyAlert in, Telemetry out). DP-B would introduce distributed coupling and reconciliation complexity. DP-C introduces manual process risk and traceability gaps incompatible with high safety criticality.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is available via standardized Tasking interface with sufficient fidelity to drive requirement derivation.
Assumption: Safety constraints are communicated via SafetyAlert interface and are stable during a derivation cycle.

Risk: Derivation rules may be incomplete or incorrect if worksite domain knowledge is not fully captured in rule set; mitigation: require domain expert review and validation of rule set before deployment.
Risk: Baseline history may grow unbounded; mitigation: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
Risk: High safety criticality demands that derivation logic be auditable and traceable; DP-A supports this via centralized implementation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: No revision recommended at DP level. DP-A is well-scoped, exhibits low coupling, and aligns with high safety criticality by centralizing derivation logic and audit trail. Proceed to IM assignment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the performance analytics engine, derivation rule set, requirement recording and traceability system, and baseline history management. This module is the system-level integrator and is responsible for accepting worksite mission inputs, applying derivation logic, and exposing derived requirements and baseline history to stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (task type, site conditions, objectives, constraints) from Site Manager or planning system; used as input to requirement derivation algorithm.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services; used to inform and validate performance requirement derivation, especially availability and safety-critical cycle time targets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance requirements, baseline history, and audit trail to stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality); also available for Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to consume for operational planning.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for NGHE Solution to receive Tasking and SafetyAlert inputs and transmit Telemetry outputs; ensures baseline history and audit trail are accessible across distributed stakeholders.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integrator and natural owner of cross-cutting performance analytics and baseline management. Derivation logic, recording, and history management are cohesive and should not be diffused. High containment score reflects that all three sub-functions (derive, record, manage) are tightly coupled and belong in a single module. Diffusion score is minimal (5%) accounting only for external interface dependencies (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link).

Vehicle module is responsible for vehicle-level control and operation, not system-level performance analytics. Placing derivation logic in Vehicle would fragment the requirement baseline across vehicle instances and complicate audit trail. High diffusion score reflects that performance analytics is orthogonal to vehicle control.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and tele-operator interface, not performance requirement derivation. Placing analytics in Teleoperation would couple requirement management to operator session lifecycle and complicate baseline history. High diffusion score reflects that performance analytics is orthogonal to teleoperation control.

Safety Services is responsible for safety monitoring and alert generation, not performance requirement derivation. While Safety Services provides safety constraints as input (SafetyAlert interface), the derivation engine itself should remain in NGHE Solution to maintain separation of concerns and avoid coupling safety monitoring to requirement analytics. High diffusion score reflects that performance analytics is a distinct concern from safety services.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The performance analytics engine, derivation rule set, requirement recording system, and baseline history management are best contained within the NGHE Solution module because: (1) they are system-level concerns that integrate inputs from multiple sources (Tasking, SafetyAlert) and serve multiple stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality); (2) centralizing these functions ensures a single source of truth for performance baselines and audit trail, critical for high safety criticality; (3) the module boundary aligns with stakeholder responsibilities (System Engineer owns NGHE Solution) and supports traceability requirements.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a monolith if performance analytics grows to include vehicle-specific or tele-operator-specific derivation logic; mitigation: establish clear separation between system-level analytics (in NGHE Solution) and vehicle/teleoperation-specific performance tuning (in Vehicle/Teleoperation modules).
Risk: Baseline history may become a bottleneck if accessed by many stakeholders concurrently; mitigation: implement caching and read-only access patterns for baseline queries.
Risk: Derivation rule set may be difficult to maintain if rules are hard-coded; mitigation: implement rule engine with externalized rule definitions and version control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended at IM level. NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner with high containment score (95%) and low diffusion risk. Proceed to gate review.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized performance analytics engine) directly and completely addresses the refined FR. The DP specifies how to derive requirements from worksite mission context (derivation rules applied to Tasking inputs), how to record them with traceability (metadata capture and storage), and how to manage baselines (versioned repository with audit trail). The DP exhibits low coupling and is appropriate for high safety criticality. No FR-DP mismatch detected.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk: NGHE Solution is the natural owner with 95% containment score. No logic is diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: DP-A is fully contained within NGHE Solution module. All external dependencies (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are well-defined interfaces. No hidden or implicit dependencies detected. Ownership is clear: System Engineer owns NGHE Solution and is accountable for performance analytics implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) Derivation algorithm can be tested with synthetic worksite mission inputs and validated against expected performance targets; (2) Traceability can be verified by inspecting recorded metadata; (3) Baseline history can be audited by querying the managed repository; (4) Stakeholder access can be verified via Telemetry interface. All verification activities are within scope of NGHE Solution module testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution module. Sub-FRs (derive, record, manage) are all allocated to the same module, ensuring coherent implementation and testing. Stakeholder responsibilities are clear: Site Manager and Safety Manager provide inputs (Tasking, SafetyAlert); System Engineer implements analytics; Maintenance/Quality consumes baseline history via Telemetry.

recommended_revisions: Establish a detailed rule set specification for performance requirement derivation, including task types, site conditions, safety constraints, and derivation algorithms. This specification should be reviewed and approved by domain experts (Site Manager, Safety Manager) before implementation.
Define a baseline history retention and archival policy to prevent unbounded growth of the managed repository.
Specify the format and content of traceability metadata to ensure auditability and compliance with safety criticality requirements.

decision.justification: The refined FR is not atomic and has been decomposed into three sub-FRs (derive, record, manage). Each sub-FR is a distinct functional concern that warrants further decomposition to the next level. The DP and IM assignments are sound and pass all gates, but the sub-FRs require detailed design at the next level to specify algorithms, data structures, interfaces, and implementation details. Proceeding with ZIGZAG_DOWN allows each sub-FR to be refined independently while maintaining traceability to CR-NGHE-005 and the NGHE Solution module boundary.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context inputs
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and derivation method
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, calibration updates, and requirement revisions with audit trail

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available via standardized Tasking interface with sufficient fidelity to drive requirement derivation.
Safety constraints are communicated via SafetyAlert interface and are stable during a derivation cycle.
System Engineer owns NGHE Solution module and is accountable for performance analytics implementation.
Site Manager and Safety Manager are primary stakeholders for derived requirements and baseline history.
Derivation rules can be captured in a rule set and are not subject to real-time changes during operation.
open_risks: Derivation rules may be incomplete or incorrect if worksite domain knowledge is not fully captured; mitigation: require domain expert review and validation of rule set before deployment.
Baseline history may grow unbounded; mitigation: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
High safety criticality demands that derivation logic be auditable and traceable; mitigation: implement centralized analytics engine with comprehensive audit trail.
Performance analytics may become a bottleneck if accessed by many stakeholders concurrently; mitigation: implement caching and read-only access patterns.
Derivation rule set may be difficult to maintain if rules are hard-coded; mitigation: implement rule engine with externalized rule definitions.
evidence_needed: Detailed rule set specification for performance requirement derivation, including task types, site conditions, safety constraints, and derivation algorithms.
Baseline history retention and archival policy.
Traceability metadata format and content specification.
Performance and scalability analysis for baseline history queries under concurrent access.
Rule engine design and implementation approach (hard-coded vs. externalized rules).
Validation results from domain expert review of derivation rules.
